sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Discuss and evaluate common climate change misconceptions using structured materials and a short formative check.

content.title: Climate misconception discussion
content.purpose: Surface and challenge common misconceptions about climate change.
content.learner_task: Work through the misconception cards, complete the analysis template, and discuss using the prompt set.

materials.task_cards:
- "Cold winters disprove global warming"
- "Climate has always changed naturally"
- "The sun is solely responsible for recent warming"
- "Scientists disagree that humans are causing warming"
- "Carbon dioxide is not a pollutant so it cannot warm the planet"
- "Renewable energy cannot replace fossil fuels"
- "Climate models are unreliable"
- "Extreme weather cannot be linked to climate change"
- "Arctic ice loss is only a local issue"
- "Individual actions do not matter for global climate"
materials.analysis_template:
## Misconception analysis worksheet

Claim under review:

Evidence for / against:

- Supported by data
- Contradicted by data

Classification:

☐ Scientifically supported
☐ Misconception
☐ Uncertain — needs more evidence

Response notes:

---
discussion_prompts: Which misconception did your group find most persuasive at first, and why?
What evidence from the lecture would you use to challenge that claim?
How would you explain the difference between weather and climate to a peer?
evaluation_checklist: ☐ Each group member reviewed at least three misconception cards
☐ Analysis template completed for the focal claim
☐ Group agreed on supported vs misconception classification
☐ Discussion prompts addressed with evidence from course materials

content.expected_output:
## What you will produce

- A completed analysis template for one focal misconception
- Brief group notes citing at least two evidence sources
- A one-sentence corrected explanation suitable for peers

items.proposition: Cold winters in one region prove that global warming is not happening.

items.statement: Climate models are unreliable because they cannot predict daily weather.
